localizedDescriptions.en:
Document to communicate information to others about the result of a test or
                assessment.
Identification information about the report.

The findings and interpretation of a pathology laboratory test performed on
                        patient-related specimens.

Identification of the pathology test performed, sometimes including specimen
                        type and patient state.

annotations.comment:
A test result may be for a single analyte, or a group of items, including panel
                        tests. Coding with a terminology, potientially a pre-coordinated term including specimen type,
                        is preferred, where possible. May be coded with LOINC or Snomed-CT. Examples include "Glucose",
                        "Urea and Electrolytes", "Swab", “Cortisol (am)” or "Liver Biopsy".

localizedDescriptions.en:
To record details of a laboratory
                                                        specimen.

The type of specimen to be collected e.g
                                                        venous blood, prostatic biopsy.

The date and time that collection has been
                                                        ordered to take place or has taken place.

Any conditions to be met before the sample
                                                        should be taken.

annotations.comment:
e.g. fasting, 'full bladder', 'sterile field' or
                                                        any special instructions on the handling or immediate processing
                                                        of the sample e.g. centrifuge on receipt.

                                                        Can also be used to document any known deviations from
                                                        collection or handling instructions e.g patient was not fasted.

localizedDescriptions.en:
The method of collection to be used eg
                                                        Venepuncture, biopsy, resection.

Additional detailed description of method of
                                                        sample collection.

Any risk or biohazard associated with
                                                        collecting or handling the specimen.

Identification of the setting at which the
                                                        specimen was collected
                                                        from a subject of care. The specimen is often collected by a
                                                        healthcare provider, but may be collected directly by the
                                                        patient or carer at home.

annotations.comment:
This specifies the specimen collection location
                                                        within the healthcare
                                                        environment. It enables the laboratory to ask questions about
                                                        the
                                                        collection of the specimen, if required. The specimen collection
                                                        setting may provide additional information relevant to the
                                                        analysis
                                                        of the result.

localizedDescriptions.en:
Identifier of the person or agency
                                                        responsible for collecting the specimen.

The total number of containers holding this
                                                        specimen.

Details of a specimen container.
                                                                This is often integral to workflow and reporting of
                                                                pathology specimens, particularly anatomical and
                                                                histological pathology specimens.
The type of container.

The container is a tissue
                                                                cassette.

The container is a tissue microarray
                                                                cassette.
The container is a specimen vial.

The container is a microscope
                                                                slide.

The container is a gneric specimen
                                                                container.

The container is an electron
                                                                microscopy grid.
The container is a specimen well.

Any special fixative or transport
                                                                medium requirements.

A text description of the
                                                                container.

The data and time that the specimen
                                                                was uplifted for transportation to the laboratory.

The unique identifier given to the
                                                                container. May be multiple.

Identifier of the place from where
                                                                the container should be collected.

Identifier of person or agency
                                                                responsible for transporting the container to the lab.
Problems with the received specimen.
Specific issue with a received specimen.
The specimen was haemolysed.
The specimen was lipaemic.

An incorrect preservative was used when
                                                        transporting the specimen.

An insufficient sample was given to
                                                        undertake measurement.

Issue arising with handling or processing of
                                                        the specimen within the laboratory.
An error arose when handling the specimen.

The specimen was too old to analyse
                                                        accurately.

An accident occurred with the sample in the
                                                        laboratory.

The specimen could not be analysed for
                                                        technical reasons.
Is the specimen adequate for testing?

The specimen is of sufficient quality to
                                                        allow reporting.

The specimen is unsatisfactory but has been
                                                        processed.

The specimen is unsatisfactory and has not
                                                        been processed.

An additional text comment on the quality of
                                                        the received specimen.
Workflow of specimen processing/handling.

The date and time that the sample was
                                                        received at the laboratory.

Unique identifier of the specimen, normally
                                                        assigned by the laboratory.
Details of a related specimen.

Unique identifier of the parent specimen,
                                                        where the specimen is split into sub-samples.
The status of the laboratory test result as a whole.

The existence of the test is registered in the Laboratory Information
                        System, but there is nothing yet available.

This is a partial (e.g. initial, interim or preliminary) Test Result: data
                        in the Test Result may be incomplete or unverified.
The Test result is complete and verified by an authorised person.

The result has been modified subsequent to being Final, and is complete and
                        verified by the responsible pathologist.

The result has been modified subsequent to being Final, and is complete and
                        verified by the responsible pathologist, and result data has been changed.

The result is unavailable because the test was not started or not completed
                        (also sometimes called 'aborted').
The Test Result has been withdrawn following previous Final release.

The date and/or time that the entire result was issued for the recorded
                        ‘Test Report Status’.
The diagnostic service that performs the examination.

annotations.comment:
For example, biochemistry, haematology. This helps clinicians filter for broad
                        categories of results accoridng to local laboratory arrangements. Coding with a terminology is
                        desirable, where possible.

localizedDescriptions.en:
Description of clinical information available at the time of interpretation
                        of results, which may include a link to the original clinical information provided in the test
                        request.

annotations.comment:
In HL7 fhir this information is held within the linked requestDetail.clinical
                        Notes.

localizedDescriptions.en:
Laboratory test result as a single value or
                                                        in a panel/battery format common to clinical pathology testing.

Specific detailed result, including both the
                                                        value of the result item, and additional information that may be
                                                        useful for clinical interpretation.

Actual value of the result. The name of this
                                                        element is normally replaced in a template or at run-time to
                                                        relfect the actual analyte e.g. Serum sodium and will often be
                                                        coded.
Comment about the Result.

Additional advice on the applicability of
                                                        the reference range.
The status of the result value.
No result yet available.

This is an initial or interim result: data
                                                        may be missing or verification not been performed.

The result is complete and verified by the
                                                        responsible pathologist.

The result has been modified subsequent to
                                                        being Final, and is complete and verified by the responsible
                                                        pathologist.

The result is unavailable because the test
                                                        was not started or not completed.

The result is not available as the test was
                                                        not requested.

The date and/or time that the entire result
                                                        was issued for the recorded ‘Result status’.

Concise and clinically contextualised narrative interpretation of the
                        laboratory test results.

Single word, phrase or brief description representing the narrative in the
                        'Conclusion'.

annotations.comment: Coding with a terminology is preferred, where possible.

localizedDescriptions.en: Additional narrative about the test not captured in other fields.

Rich text or other multimedia representation of the entire result as issued
                        by the diagnostic service.

annotations.comment: Multiple formats are allowed but they must be semantically equivalent.

localizedDescriptions.en:
Aspects of the condition or circumstances of the patient that may alter
                        interpretation of the laboratory test.

The local identifier given to the laboratory test result by the Laboratory
                        Information System.
Details concerning a single laboratory test requested.

Identification of the original laboratory test requested, where the test
                        requested differs from the test actually performed.
The local ID assigned to the test order by the order requester.

annotations.comment: Equivalent to the HL7 Placer Order Identifier.

localizedDescriptions.en:
The local ID assigned to the test order by the order filler, usually by the
                        Laboratory Information System (LIS).

annotations.comment:
Assigning an identifier to a request by the Laboratory lnformation System (LIS)
                        enables tracking progress of the request and enables linking results to requests. It also
                        provides a reference to assist with enquiries and it is usually equivalent to the HL7 Filler
                        Order Identifier.

Usually there is one test request (or "order") for each result, however in some
                        circumstances multiple test requests may be represented using a single Pathology test result
                        archetype.

localizedDescriptions.en:
True if a point of care device supplies the test result rather than a formal
                        laboratory service.

annotations.comment:
This archetype may be used to record a single valued test, but will often be
                        specialised or templated to represent multiple value or 'panel' tests. This archetype also acts
                        as the parent for specialisations appropriate for more specific laboratory tests, e.g.
                        microbiology, histopathology.
